title: ranunculus auricomus subsp. montserratii
intro: Ranunculus auricomus subsp. montserratii, also known as the Goldilocks buttercup, is a subspecies of the greater celandine plant (Ranunculus auricomus) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms, including fungi and bacteria. The health benefits of Ranunculus auricomus subsp. montserratii include its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into tinctures, infusions, or salves, and are often used topically to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
